questions.question: What triggered this work?
questions.answer: Current MCP server only exposes 1 tool (review_code) out of 30+ WFC skills. Multi-tenant per-project doesn't match reality (1 developer = 1 machine). .wfc/ directories scattered per-project cause inconsistency. Need centralized ~/.wfc/ for predictable behavior.

questions.question: Who are the primary users/stakeholders?
questions.answer: Individual developers using Claude Desktop, Claude Code, and Claude IDE with WFC. Development teams wanting consistent WFC behavior across team members. Developer acts as PO/Lead Architect of their AI pod.

questions.question: What's the current state?
questions.answer: MCP server at wfc/servers/mcp_server.py (340 lines). Exposes 1 tool (review_code), 2 resources. Multi-tenant per-project with ProjectContext. API key per project (SHA-256). Rate limiting via TokenBucket (10 req/sec). WorktreePool (max 10). 9/9 tests passing.

questions.question: What's the desired outcome - what does done look like?
questions.answer: All 30+ WFC skills exposed as MCP tools. Centralized ~/.wfc/ directory (human-readable structure). Single WFC instance per developer machine. No filesystem collisions (branches get folders, plans get folders). Hybrid knowledge (global + project-specific). Per-developer API key. Auto-detection from git repo root.

questions.question: What MUST this do (non-negotiable)?
questions.answer: M1: All 30+ skills as MCP tools. M2: Centralized ~/.wfc/ with human-readable names. M3: Zero FS collisions. M4: Auto-detect from git. M5: Per-developer auth. M6: Migration from current.
acceptance_criteria: 3 plans on different branches coexist without conflicts
Switch branches, outputs route to correct folder
Single API key works across all repos
